other_observers: Brandon Percival, Mark Peterson, Brad Steger, Kara Lewantowicz, Nick ? from Cheyenne Mtn High School; Kara Lewantowicz from the A.M. of the 16th although a local was rumored to have seen the bird there for a week.
specific_location: Hansen Nature Park, the southern end of Fountain Creek Regional Park
description: This was a chunky and short-legged blue-gray heron. It had dull yellowish tibio-tarsi, a wide dark gray-black bill, a blackish head that had a distince narrow rectangular white cheek. The longish neck was concolorous with the body, more dark grayish with bluish cast. The crown was creamy white with long filoplumes sticking straight out behind its head. The orbit was bright red in good light.Behaviors: Mostly solitary at the edge of a cattail marsh, but it did fly up about 18 feet into a leafless tree for about 15 minutes before flying down to the water at the edge of the marsh. It was hunting, but i did not see it catch anything although there was some organic matter sticking out of its bill.Call: none heardPlumage: adult non-breeding
